configutils.Unset]:\n1382:         self._basic_py_validation(value, dict)\n1383:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1384:             return value\n1385:         elif not value:\n1386:             return self._fill_fixed_keys({})\n1387: \n1388:         self._validate_keys(value)\n1389:         for key, val in value.items():\n1390:             self._validate_surrogate_escapes(value, key)\n1391:             self._validate_surrogate_escapes(value, val)\n1392: \n1393:         d = {self.keytype.to_py(key): self.valtype.to_py(val)\n1394:              for key, val in value.items()}\n1395:         return self._fill_fixed_keys(d)\n1396: \n1397:     def to_str(self, value: typing.Dict) -> str:\n1398:         if not value:\n1399:             # An empty Dict is treated just like None -> empty string\n1400:             return ''\n1401:         return json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True)\n1402: \n1403:     def to_doc(self, value: typing.Dict, indent: int = 0) -> str:\n1404:         if not value:\n1405:             return 'empty'\n1406:         lines = ['\\n']\n1407:         prefix = '-' if not indent else '*' * indent\n1408:         for key, val in sorted(value.items()):\n1409:             lines += ('{} {}: {}'.format(\n1410:                 prefix,\n1411:                 self.keytype.to_doc(key),\n1412:                 self.valtype.to_doc(val, indent=indent+1),\n1413:             )).splitlines()\n1414:         return '\\n'.join(line.rstrip(' ') for line in lines)\n1415: \n1416: \n1417: class File(BaseType):\n1418: \n1419:     \"\"\"A file on the local filesystem.\"\"\"\n1420: \n1421:     def __init__(self, required: bool = True, **kwargs: typing.Any) -> None:\n1422:         super().__init__(**kwargs)\n1423:         self.required = required\n1424: \n1425:     def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n1426:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1427:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1428:             return value\n1429:         elif not value:\n1430:             return None\n1431: \n1432:         value = os.path.expanduser(value)\n1433:         value = os.path.expandvars(value)\n1434:         try:\n1435:             if not os.path.isabs(value):\n1436:                 value = os.path.join(standarddir.config(), value)\n1437: \n1438:             if self.required and not os.path.isfile(value):\n1439:                 raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1440:                     value,\n1441:                     \"Must be an existing file (absolute or relative to the \"\n1442:                     \"config directory)!\")\n1443:         except UnicodeEncodeError as e:\n1444:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, e)\n1445: \n1446:         return value\n1447: \n1448: \n1449: class Directory(BaseType):\n1450: \n1451:     \"\"\"A directory on the local filesystem.\"\"\"\n1452: \n1453:     def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n1454:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1455:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1456:             return value\n1457:         elif not value:\n1458:             return None\n1459:         value = os.path.expandvars(value)\n1460:         value = os.path.expanduser(value)\n1461:         try:\n1462:             if not os.path.isdir(value):\n1463:                 raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1464:                     value, \"must be a valid directory!\")\n1465:             if not os.path.isabs(value):\n1466:                 raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1467:                     value, \"must be an absolute path!\")\n1468:         except UnicodeEncodeError as e:\n1469:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, e)\n1470: \n1471:         return value\n1472: \n1473: \n1474: class FormatString(BaseType):\n1475: \n1476:     \"\"\"A string with placeholders.\"\"\"\n1477: \n1478:     def __init__(self, fields: typing.Iterable[str],\n1479:                  none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n1480:         super().__init__(none_ok)\n1481:         self.fields = fields\n1482: \n1483:     def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n1484:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1485:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1486:             return value\n1487:         elif not value:\n1488:             return None\n1489: \n1490:         try:\n1491:             value.format(**{k: '' for k in self.fields})\n1492:         except (KeyError, IndexError) as e:\n1493:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"Invalid placeholder \"\n1494:                                             \"{}\".format(e))\n1495:         except ValueError as e:\n1496:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, str(e))\n1497: \n1498:         return value\n1499: \n1500: \n1501: class ShellCommand(List):\n1502: \n1503:     \"\"\"A shell command as a list.\n1504: \n1505:     See the documentation for `List`.\n1506: \n1507:     Attributes:\n1508:         placeholder: If there should be a placeholder.\n1509:     \"\"\"\n1510: \n1511:     _show_valtype = False\n1512: \n1513:     def __init__(self, placeholder: bool = False,\n1514:                  none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n1515:         super().__init__(valtype=String(), none_ok=none_ok)\n1516:         self.placeholder = placeholder\n1517: \n1518:     def to_py(\n1519:             self,\n1520:             value: typing.Union[typing.List, configutils.Unset],\n1521:     ) -> typing.Union[typing.List, configutils.Unset]:\n1522:         py_value = super().to_py(value)\n1523:         if isinstance(py_value, configutils.Unset):\n1524:             return py_value\n1525:         elif not py_value:\n1526:             return []\n1527: \n1528:         if (self.placeholder and\n1529:                 '{}' not in ' '.join(py_value) and\n1530:                 '{file}' not in ' '.join(py_value)):\n1531:             raise configexc.ValidationError(py_value, \"needs to contain a \"\n1532:                                             \"{}-placeholder or a \"\n1533:                                             \"{file}-placeholder.\")\n1534:         return py_value\n1535: \n1536: \n1537: class Proxy(BaseType):\n1538: \n1539:     \"\"\"A proxy URL, or `system`/`none`.\"\"\"\n1540: \n1541:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n1542:         super().__init__(none_ok)\n1543:         self.valid_values = ValidValues(\n1544:             ('system', \"Use the system wide proxy.\"),\n1545:             ('none', \"Don't use any proxy\"))\n1546: \n1547:     def to_py(\n1548:             self,\n1549:             value: _StrUnset\n1550:     ) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset, None, QNetworkProxy, _SystemProxy]:\n1551:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1552:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1553:             return value\n1554:         elif not value:\n1555:             return None\n1556: \n1557:         try:\n1558:             if value == 'system':\n1559:                 return SYSTEM_PROXY\n1560: \n1561:             if value == 'none':\n1562:                 url = QUrl('direct://')\n1563:             else:\n1564:                 # If we add a special value to valid_values, we need to handle\n1565:                 # it here!\n1566:                 assert self.valid_values is not None\n1567:                 assert value not in self.valid_values, value\n1568:                 url = QUrl(value)\n1569:             return urlutils.proxy_from_url(url)\n1570:         except (urlutils.InvalidUrlError, urlutils.InvalidProxyTypeError) as e:\n1571:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, e)\n1572: \n1573:     def complete(self) -> _Completions:\n1574:         assert self.valid_values is not None\n1575:         out = []\n1576:         for val in self.valid_values:\n1577:             out.append((val, self.valid_values.descriptions[val]))\n1578:         out.append(('http://', 'HTTP proxy URL'))\n1579:         out.append(('socks://', 'SOCKS proxy URL'))\n1580:         out.append(('socks://localhost:9050/', 'Tor via SOCKS'))\n1581:         out.append(('http://localhost:8080/', 'Local HTTP proxy'))\n1582:         out.append(('pac+https://example.com/proxy.pac', 'Proxy autoconfiguration file URL'))\n1583:         return out\n1584: \n1585: \n1586: class SearchEngineUrl(BaseType):\n1587: \n1588:     \"\"\"A search engine URL.\"\"\"\n1589: \n1590:     def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n1591:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1592:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1593:             return value\n1594:         elif not value:\n1595:             return None\n1596: \n1597:         if not ('{}' in value or '{0}' in value):\n1598:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must contain \\\"{}\\\"\")\n1599: \n1600:         try:\n1601:             value.format(\"\")\n1602:         except (KeyError, IndexError):\n1603:             raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1604:                 value, \"may not contain {...} (use {{ and }} for literal {/})\")\n1605:         except ValueError as e:\n1606:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, str(e))\n1607: \n1608:         url = QUrl(value.replace('{}', 'foobar'))\n1609:         if not url.isValid():\n1610:             raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1611:                 value, \"invalid url, {}\".format(url.errorString()))\n1612: \n1613:         return value\n1614: \n1615: \n1616: class FuzzyUrl(BaseType):\n1617: \n1618:     \"\"\"A URL which gets interpreted as search if needed.\"\"\"\n1619: \n1620:     def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n1621:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1622:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1623:             return value\n1624:         elif not value:\n1625:             return None\n1626: \n1627:         try:\n1628:             return urlutils.fuzzy_url(value, do_search=False)\n1629:         except urlutils.InvalidUrlError as e:\n1630:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, str(e))\n1631: \n1632: \n1633: @attr.s\n1634: class PaddingValues:\n1635: \n1636:     \"\"\"Four padding values.\"\"\"\n1637: \n1638:     top = attr.ib()  # type: int\n1639:     bottom = attr.ib()  # type: int\n1640:     left = attr.ib()  # type: int\n1641:     right = attr.ib()  # type: int\n1642: \n1643: \n1644: class Padding(Dict):\n1645: \n1646:     \"\"\"Setting for paddings around elements.\"\"\"\n1647: \n1648:     _show_valtype = False\n1649: \n1650:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n1651:         super().__init__(keytype=String(),\n1652:                          valtype=Int(minval=0, none_ok=none_ok),\n1653:                          fixed_keys=['top', 'bottom', 'left', 'right'],\n1654:                          none_ok=none_ok)\n1655: \n1656:     def to_py(  # type: ignore\n1657:             self,\n1658:             value: typing.Union[configutils.Unset, typing.Dict, None],\n1659:     ) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset, PaddingValues]:\n1660:         d = super().to_py(value)\n1661:         if isinstance(d, configutils.Unset):\n1662:             return d\n1663: \n1664:         return PaddingValues(**d)\n1665: \n1666: \n1667: class Encoding(BaseType):\n1668: \n1669:     \"\"\"Setting for a python encoding.\"\"\"\n1670: \n1671:     def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n1672:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1673:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1674:             return value\n1675:         elif not value:\n1676:             return None\n1677:         try:\n1678:             codecs.lookup(value)\n1679:         except LookupError:\n1680:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"is not a valid encoding!\")\n1681:         return value\n1682: \n1683: \n1684: class Position(MappingType):\n1685: \n1686:     \"\"\"The position of the tab bar.\"\"\"\n1687: \n1688:     MAPPING = {\n1689:         'top': QTabWidget.North,\n1690:         'bottom': QTabWidget.South,\n1691:         'left': QTabWidget.West,\n1692:         'right': QTabWidget.East,\n1693:     }\n1694: \n1695:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n1696:         super().__init__(\n1697:             none_ok,\n1698:             valid_values=ValidValues('top', 'bottom', 'left', 'right'))\n1699: \n1700: \n1701: class TextAlignment(MappingType):\n1702: \n1703: \n1711:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n1712:         super().__init__(\n1713:             none_ok,\n1714:             valid_values=ValidValues('left', 'right', 'center'))\n1715: \n1716: \n1717: class VerticalPosition(String):\n1718: \n1719:     \"\"\"The position of the download bar.\"\"\"\n1720: \n1721:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> string.\"\"\"\n1729: \n1730:     def to_py(\n1731:             self,\n1732:             value: _StrUnset\n1733:     ) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset, None, QUrl]:\n1734: return value\n1760: \n1761: \n1762: class SelectOnRemove(MappingType):\n1763: \n1764:     \"\"\"Which tab to select when the focused tab is removed.\"\"\"\n1765: \n1766:     MAPPING = {\n1767:         'prev': QTabBar.SelectLeftTab,\n1768:         'next': QTabBar.SelectRightTab,\n1769:         'last-used': QTabBar.SelectPreviousTab,\n1770:     }\n1771: \n1772:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n1773:         super().__init__(\n1774:             none_ok,\n1775:             valid_values=ValidValues(\n1776:                 ('prev', \"Select the tab which came before the closed one \"\n1777:                  \"(left in horizontal, above in vertical).\"),\n1778:                 ('next', \"Select the tab which came after the closed one \"\n1779:                  \"(right in horizontal, below in vertical).\"),\n1780:                 ('last-used', \"Select the previously selected tab.\")))\n1781: \n1782: \n1783: class ConfirmQuit(FlagList):\n1784: \n1785:     \"\"\"Whether to display a confirmation when the window is closed.\"\"\"\n1786: \n1787:     # Values that can be combined with commas\n1788:     combinable_values = ('multiple-tabs', 'downloads')\n1789: \n1790:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n1791:         super().__init__(none_ok)\n1792:         self.valtype.none_ok = none_ok\n1793:         self.valtype.valid_values = ValidValues(\n1794:             ('always', \"Always show a confirmation.\"),\n1795:             ('multiple-tabs', \"Show a confirmation if \"\n1796:              \"multiple tabs are opened.\"),\n1797:             ('downloads', \"Show a confirmation if \"\n1798:              \"downloads are running\"),\n1799:             ('never', \"Never show a confirmation.\"))\n1800: \n1801:     def to_py(\n1802:             self,\n1803:             value: typing.Union[configutils.Unset, typing.List],\n1804:     ) -> typing.Union[typing.List, configutils.Unset]:\n1805:         values = super().to_py(value)\n1806:         if isinstance(values, configutils.Unset):\n1807:             return values\n1808:         elif not values:\n1809:             return []\n1810: \n1811:         # Never can't be set with other options\n1812:         if 'never' in values and len(values) > 1:\n1813:             raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1814:                 values, \"List cannot contain never!\")\n1815:         # Always can't be set with other options\n1816:         elif 'always' in values and len(values) > 1:\n1817:             raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1818:                 values, \"List cannot contain always!\")\n1819: \n1820:         return values\n1821: \n1822: \n1823: class NewTabPosition(String):\n1824: \n1825:     \"\"\"How new tabs are positioned.\"\"\"\n1826: \n1827:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n1828:         super().__init__(none_ok=none_ok)\n1829:         self.valid_values = ValidValues(\n1830:             ('prev', \"Before the current tab.\"),\n1831:             ('next', \"After the current tab.\"),\n1832:             ('first', \"At the beginning.\"),\n1833:             ('last', \"At the end.\"))\n1834: \n1835: \n1836: class TimestampTemplate(BaseType):\n1837: \n1838:     \"\"\"An strftime-like template for timestamps.\n1839: \n1840:     See https://sqlite.org/lang_datefunc.html for reference.\n1841:     \"\"\"\n1842: \n1843:     def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n1844:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1845:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1846:             return value\n1847:         elif not value:\n1848:             return None\n1849: \n1850:         try:\n1851:             # Dummy check to see if the template is valid\n1852:             datetime.datetime.now().strftime(value)\n1853:         except ValueError as error:\n1854:             # thrown on invalid template string\n1855:             raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1856:                 value, \"Invalid format string: {}\".format(error))\n1857: \n1858:         return value\n1859: \n1860: \n1861: class Key(BaseType):\n1862: \n1863:     \"\"\"A name of a key.\"\"\"\n1864: \n1865:     def from_obj(self, value: str) -> str:\n1866:         \"\"\"Make sure key sequences are always normalized.\"\"\"\n1867:         return str(keyutils.KeySequence.parse(value))\n1868: \n1869:     def to_py(\n1870:             self,\n1871:             value: _StrUnset\n1872:     ) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset, None, keyutils.KeySequence]:\n1873:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1874:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1875:             return value\n1876:         elif not value:\n1877:             return None\n1878: \n1879:         try:\n1880:             return keyutils.KeySequence.parse(value)\n1881:         except keyutils.KeyParseError as e:\n1882:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, str(e))\n1883: \n1884: \n1885: class UrlPattern(BaseType):\n1886: \n1887:     \"\"\"A match pattern for a URL.\n1888: \n1889:     See https://developer.chrome.com/apps/match_patterns for the allowed\n1890:     syntax.\n1891:     \"\"\"\n1892: \n1893:     def to_py(\n1894:             self,\n1895:             value: _StrUnset\n1896:     ) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset, None, urlmatch.UrlPattern]:\n1897:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1898:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n1899:             return value\n1900:         elif not value:\n1901:             return None\n1902: \n1903:         try:\n1904:             return urlmatch.UrlPattern(value)\n1905:         except urlmatch.ParseError as e:\n1906:             raise configexc.ValidationError(value, str(e))\n1907: ",

`#RGB`/`#RRGGBB`/`#RRRGGGBBB`/`#RRRRGGGGBBBB`\\n997:     * An SVG color name as specified in\\n998:       http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/types.html#ColorKeywords[the W3C specification].\\n999:     * transparent (no color)\\n1000:     * `rgb(r, g, b)` / `rgba(r, g, b, a)` (values 0-255 or percentages)\\n1001:     * `hsv(h, s, v)` / `hsva(h, s, v, a)` (values 0-255, hue 0-359)\\n1002: pass\\n1009: \\n1010:         mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\\n1011:         if val.endswith('%'):\\n1012:             val = val[:-1]\\n1013:             mult = mult / 100\\n1014: \\n1015:         try:\\n1016:             return int(float(val) * mult)\\n1017:         except ValueError:\\n1018:             raise configexc.ValidationError(val, \\\"must be a valid color value\\\")\\n1019: \\n1020:     def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset,\\n1021:                                                       None, QColor]:\\n1022: \\n1028:         if '(' in value and value.endswith(')'):\\n1029:             openparen = value.index('(')\\n1030:             kind = value[:openparen]\\n1031:             vals = value[openparen+1:-1].split(',')\\n1032: \\n1033:             converters = {\\n1034:                 'rgba': QColor.fromRgb,\\n1035:                 'rgb': QColor.fromRgb,\\n1036:                 'hsva': QColor.fromHsv,\\n1037:                 'hsv': QColor.fromHsv,\\n1038:             }\\n1039: \\n1040:             conv = converters.get(kind)\\n1041:             if not conv:\\n1042: transparent (no color)\n1000:     * `rgb(r, g, b)` / `rgba(r, g, b, a)` (values 0-255 or percentages)\n1001:     * `hsv(h, s, v)` / `hsva(h, s, v, a)` (values 0-255, hue 0-359)\n1002:         conv = converters.get(kind)\\n1133:             if not conv:\\n1134:                 raise configexc.ValidationError(\\n1135:                     value,\\n1136:                     '{} not in {}'.format(kind, sorted(converters)))\\n1137: \\n1138:             if len(kind) != len(vals):\\n1139:                 raise configexc.ValidationError(\\n1140:                     value,\\n1141:                     'expected {} values for {}'.format(len(kind), kind))\\n1142: \\n1143:             int_vals = [self._parse_value(kind, val)\\n1144:                         for kind, val in zip(kind, vals)]\\n1145:             return conv(*int_vals)\\n1146: \\n1147:         color = QColor(value)\\n1148:         if color.isValid():\\n1149:             return color\\n1150:         else:\\n1151: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser@45bcc7833d11b5fdef6d75b117c897173a9af0f6/-/blob/qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py%3FL1090-1150\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1090:     * An SVG color name as specified in\n1091:       https://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/types.html#ColorKeywords[the W3C specification].\n1092:     * transparent (no color)\n1093:     * `rgb(r, g, b)` / `rgba(r, g, b, a)` (values 0-255 or percentages)\n1094:     * `hsv(h, s, v)` / `hsva(h, s, v, a)` (values 0-255, hue 0-359)\n1095:     \"\"\"\n1096: \n1097:     def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n1098:         try:\n1099:             return int(val)\n1100:         except ValueError:\n1101:             pass\n1102: \n1103:         mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n1104:         if val.endswith('%'):\n1105:             val = val[:-1]\n1106:             mult /= 100\n1107: \n1108:         try:\n1109:             return int(float(val) * mult)\n1110:         except ValueError:\n1111:             raise configexc.ValidationError(val, \"must be a valid color value\")\n1112: \n1113:     def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> Union[_UnsetNone, QColor]:\n1114:         self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n1115:         if isinstance(value, usertypes.Unset):\n1116:             return value\n1117:         elif not value:\n1118:             return None\n1119: \n1120:         if '(' in value and value.endswith(')'):\n1121:             openparen = value.index('(')\n1122:             kind = value[:openparen]\n1123:             vals = value[openparen+1:-1].split(',')\n1124: \n1125:             converters: dict[str, Callable[..., QColor]] = {\n1126:                 'rgba': QColor.fromRgb,\n1127:                 'rgb': QColor.fromRgb,\n1128:                 'hsva': QColor.fromHsv,\n1129:                 'hsv': QColor.fromHsv,\n1130:             }\n1131: \n1132:             conv = converters.get(kind)\n1133:             if not conv:\n1134:                 raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1135:                     value,\n1136:                     '{} not in {}'.format(kind, sorted(converters)))\n1137: \n1138:             if len(kind) != len(vals):\n1139:                 raise configexc.ValidationError(\n1140:                     value,\n1141:                     'expected {} values for {}'.format(len(kind), kind))\n1142: \n1143:             int_vals = [self._parse_value(kind, val)\n1144:                         for kind, val in zip(kind, vals)]\n1145:             return conv(*int_vals)\n1146: \n1147:         color = QColor(value)\n1148:         if color.isValid():\n1149:             return color\n1150:         else:\n1151: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser@45bcc7833d11b5fdef6d75b117c897173a9af0f6/-/blob/qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py%3FL1090-1150"
  }
}

            "content": "1: Change Log\n2: ===========\n3: \n4: ",

          {
            "content": "370: - The security fix in v1.3.3 caused URLs with ampersands\n371:   (`www.example.com?one=1&two=2`) to send the wrong arguments when clicked on\n372:   the `qute://history` page.\n373: ",
            "endLine": 373,

            "content": "1670: - New `current` hinting mode which forces opening hints in the current tab\n1671:   (even with `target=\"_blank\"`)\n1672: \n1673: ",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1097:     def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\\n1098:         try:\\n1099:             return int(val)\\n1100:         except ValueError:\\n1101:             pass\\n1102: \\n1103:         mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\\n1104:         if val.endswith('%'):\\n1105:             val = val[:-1]\\n1106:             mult /= 100\\n1107: \\n1108:         try:\\n1109:             return int(float(val) * mult)\\n1110:         except ValueError:\\n1111:             raise configexc.ValidationError(val, \\\"must be a valid color value\\\")\\n1112: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser@45bcc7833d11b5fdef6d75b117c897173a9af0f6/-/blob/qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py%3FL1097-1111\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1097:     def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n1098:         try:\n1099:             return int(val)\n1100:         except ValueError:\n1101:             pass\n1102: \n1103:         mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n1104:         if val.endswith('%'):\n1105:             val = val[:-1]\n1106:             mult /= 100\n1107: \n1108:         try:\n1109:             return int(float(val) * mult)\n1110:         except ValueError:\n1111:             raise configexc.ValidationError(val, \"must be a valid color value\")\n1112: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/qutebrowser/qutebrowser@45bcc7833d11b5fdef6d75b117c897173a9af0f6/-/blob/qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py%3FL1097-1111"
  }
}

27. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "\"\"\"Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.\n\nThis test verifies that HSV colors with percentage values are parsed correctly,\nwith hue scaling to 0-359 degrees instead of 0-255.\n\nThe bug is that `hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)` is parsed as `(255, 255, 255)` instead\nof `(359, 255, 255)`. This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport os\n\n# Add the qutebrowser directory to the path so we can import modules\nsys.path.insert(0, '/app')\n\nimport pytest\nfrom PyQt5.QtGui import QColor\n\n# Import the color type handler\nfrom qutebrowser.config.configtypes import QtColor\n\n\nclass TestHSVPercentageScaling:\n    \"\"\"Test that HSV colors with percentages scale hue correctly.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_hsv_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 100% hue maps to 359 degrees, not 255.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 100% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 255 (100% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 359 (100% of 359)\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected hue=359 for 100%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 255, f\"Expected saturation=255, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 255, f\"Expected value=255, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 50% hue maps to ~179 degrees, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 50% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%, 50%, 50%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 127 (50% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 179 (50% of 359)\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected hue=179 for 50%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 127 or s == 128, f\"Expected saturation~127, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 127 or v == 128, f\"Expected value~127, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_10_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 10% scales correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 10% values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%, 10%, 10%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 10% of 359 \u2248 35.9 \u2192 35\n        # 10% of 255 \u2248 25.5 \u2192 25\n        assert h == 35, f\"Expected hue=35 for 10%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 25 or s == 26, f\"Expected saturation~25, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 25 or v == 26, f\"Expected value~25, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that HSVA (with alpha) also scales hue correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with 100% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 255\n        # With the fix, hue should be 359\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected hue=359 for 100%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 255, f\"Expected saturation=255, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 255, f\"Expected value=255, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 255, f\"Expected alpha=255, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that HSVA with 50% hue scales correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with 50% values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(50%, 50%, 50%, 50%)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 50% of 359 \u2248 179.5 \u2192 179\n        # 50% of 255 \u2248 127.5 \u2192 127\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected hue=179 for 50%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 127 or s == 128, f\"Expected saturation~127, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 127 or v == 128, f\"Expected value~127, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 127 or a == 128, f\"Expected alpha~127, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_25_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 25% hue maps to ~89-90 degrees, not ~63-64.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 25% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(25%, 25%, 25%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 25% of 359 \u2248 89.75 \u2192 89\n        # 25% of 255 (buggy) \u2248 63.75 \u2192 63\n        assert h == 89, f\"Expected hue=89 for 25%, got {h}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_integer_values_unchanged(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values work as expected.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with integer values (0-359 for hue, 0-255 for s/v)\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(180, 128, 64)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Integer values should be used directly\n        assert h == 180, f\"Expected hue=180, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 128, f\"Expected saturation=128, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 64, f\"Expected value=64, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_integer_values_unchanged(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that integer HSVA values work as expected.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with integer values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(180, 128, 64, 200)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Integer values should be used directly\n        assert h == 180, f\"Expected hue=180, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 128, f\"Expected saturation=128, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 64, f\"Expected value=64, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 200, f\"Expected alpha=200, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_mixed_percentage_and_integer(self):\n        \"\"\"Test HSV with mixed percentage and integer values.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # This should fail with a validation error since all values must be\n        # either percentages or integers, not mixed\n        # But let's test with all percentages for correctness\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(25%, 64, 32)')\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Hue: 25% of 359 \u2248 89.75 \u2192 89\n        # Saturation: integer 64\n        # Value: integer 32\n        # (This tests mixed percentage/integer - Qt may handle this differently)\n\n\nclass TestHSVBugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that specifically demonstrate the bug vs expected behavior.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_bug_vs_correct_100_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Compare buggy vs correct parsing for 100% hue.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # This test will FAIL if hue is 255 (the bug)\n        # It will PASS if hue is 359 (the fix)\n        assert h != 255, \"BUG DETECTED: Hue is 255, should be 359!\"\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected correct hue=359, got {h}\"\n\n    def test_bug_vs_correct_50_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Compare buggy vs correct parsing for 50% hue.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%, 50%, 50%)')\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # This test will FAIL if hue is 127 (the bug)\n        # It will PASS if hue is 179 (the fix)\n        assert h != 127, \"BUG DETECTED: Hue is 127, should be 179!\"\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected correct hue=179, got {h}\"\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    pytest.main([__file__, '-v', '--tb=short'])\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/regression_test.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "\"\"\"Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.\n\nThis test verifies that HSV colors with percentage values are parsed correctly,\nwith hue scaling to 0-359 degrees instead of 0-255.\n\nThe bug is that `hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)` is parsed as `(255, 255, 255)` instead\nof `(359, 255, 255)`. This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport os\n\n# Add the qutebrowser directory to the path so we can import modules\nsys.path.insert(0, '/app')\n\nimport pytest\nfrom PyQt5.QtGui import QColor\n\n# Import the color type handler\nfrom qutebrowser.config.configtypes import QtColor\n\n\nclass TestHSVPercentageScaling:\n    \"\"\"Test that HSV colors with percentages scale hue correctly.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_hsv_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 100% hue maps to 359 degrees, not 255.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 100% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 255 (100% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 359 (100% of 359)\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected hue=359 for 100%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 255, f\"Expected saturation=255, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 255, f\"Expected value=255, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 50% hue maps to ~179 degrees, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 50% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%, 50%, 50%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 127 (50% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 179 (50% of 359)\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected hue=179 for 50%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 127 or s == 128, f\"Expected saturation~127, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 127 or v == 128, f\"Expected value~127, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_10_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 10% scales correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 10% values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%, 10%, 10%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 10% of 359 \u2248 35.9 \u2192 35\n        # 10% of 255 \u2248 25.5 \u2192 25\n        assert h == 35, f\"Expected hue=35 for 10%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 25 or s == 26, f\"Expected saturation~25, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 25 or v == 26, f\"Expected value~25, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that HSVA (with alpha) also scales hue correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with 100% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 255\n        # With the fix, hue should be 359\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected hue=359 for 100%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 255, f\"Expected saturation=255, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 255, f\"Expected value=255, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 255, f\"Expected alpha=255, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that HSVA with 50% hue scales correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with 50% values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(50%, 50%, 50%, 50%)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 50% of 359 \u2248 179.5 \u2192 179\n        # 50% of 255 \u2248 127.5 \u2192 127\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected hue=179 for 50%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 127 or s == 128, f\"Expected saturation~127, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 127 or v == 128, f\"Expected value~127, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 127 or a == 128, f\"Expected alpha~127, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_25_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 25% hue maps to ~89-90 degrees, not ~63-64.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 25% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(25%, 25%, 25%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 25% of 359 \u2248 89.75 \u2192 89\n        # 25% of 255 (buggy) \u2248 63.75 \u2192 63\n        assert h == 89, f\"Expected hue=89 for 25%, got {h}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_integer_values_unchanged(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values work as expected.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with integer values (0-359 for hue, 0-255 for s/v)\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(180, 128, 64)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Integer values should be used directly\n        assert h == 180, f\"Expected hue=180, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 128, f\"Expected saturation=128, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 64, f\"Expected value=64, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_integer_values_unchanged(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that integer HSVA values work as expected.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with integer values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(180, 128, 64, 200)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Integer values should be used directly\n        assert h == 180, f\"Expected hue=180, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 128, f\"Expected saturation=128, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 64, f\"Expected value=64, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 200, f\"Expected alpha=200, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_mixed_percentage_and_integer(self):\n        \"\"\"Test HSV with mixed percentage and integer values.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # This should fail with a validation error since all values must be\n        # either percentages or integers, not mixed\n        # But let's test with all percentages for correctness\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(25%, 64, 32)')\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Hue: 25% of 359 \u2248 89.75 \u2192 89\n        # Saturation: integer 64\n        # Value: integer 32\n        # (This tests mixed percentage/integer - Qt may handle this differently)\n\n\nclass TestHSVBugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that specifically demonstrate the bug vs expected behavior.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_bug_vs_correct_100_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Compare buggy vs correct parsing for 100% hue.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # This test will FAIL if hue is 255 (the bug)\n        # It will PASS if hue is 359 (the fix)\n        assert h != 255, \"BUG DETECTED: Hue is 255, should be 359!\"\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected correct hue=359, got {h}\"\n\n    def test_bug_vs_correct_50_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Compare buggy vs correct parsing for 50% hue.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%, 50%, 50%)')\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # This test will FAIL if hue is 127 (the bug)\n        # It will PASS if hue is 179 (the fix)\n        assert h != 127, \"BUG DETECTED: Hue is 127, should be 179!\"\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected correct hue=179, got {h}\"\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    pytest.main([__file__, '-v', '--tb=short'])\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/regression_test.py",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],

}
28. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cd /workspace && python3 -m pytest -c /dev/null --timeout=60 regression_test.py -v 2>&1 | head -150",
  "description": "Run the regression test to see the current behavior"
}
Output
