"{\"content\":\"1: # vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:\\n2: \\n3: # Copyright 2014-2019 Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>\\n4: #\\n5: # This file is part of qutebrowser.\\n6: #\\n7: # qutebrowser is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify\\n8: # it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by\\n9: # the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or\\n10: # (at your option) any later version.\\n11: #\\n12: # qutebrowser is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,\\n13: # but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of\\n14: # MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the\\n15: # GNU General Public License for more details.\\n16: #\\n17: # You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\\n18: # along with qutebrowser.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\\n19: \\n20: \\\"\\\"\\\"Exceptions related to config parsing.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n21: \\n22: import typing\\n23: import attr\\n24: \\n25: from qutebrowser.utils import jinja, usertypes\\n26: \\n27: \\n28: class Error(Exception):\\n29: \\n30:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Base exception for config-related errors.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n31: \\n32: \\n33: class NoAutoconfigError(Error):\\n34: \\n35:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised when this option can't be set in autoconfig.yml.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n36: \\n37:     def __init__(self, name: str) -> None:\\n38:         super().__init__(\\\"The {} setting can only be set in config.py!\\\"\\n39:                          .format(name))\\n40: \\n41: \\n42: class BackendError(Error):\\n43: \\n44:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised when this setting is unavailable with the current backend.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n45: \\n46:     def __init__(\\n47:             self, name: str,\\n48:             backend: usertypes.Backend,\\n49:             raw_backends: typing.Optional[typing.Mapping[str, bool]]\\n50:     ) -> None:\\n51:         if raw_backends is None or not raw_backends[backend.name]:\\n52:             msg = (\\\"The {} setting is not available with the {} backend!\\\"\\n53:                    .format(name, backend.name))\\n54:         else:\\n55:             msg = (\\\"The {} setting needs {} with the {} backend!\\\"\\n56:                    .format(name, raw_backends[backend.name], backend.name))\\n57: \\n58:         super().__init__(msg)\\n59: \\n60: \\n61: class NoPatternError(Error):\\n62: \\n63:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised when the given setting does not support URL patterns.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n64: \\n65:     def __init__(self, name: str) -> None:\\n66:         super().__init__(\\\"The {} setting does not support URL patterns!\\\"\\n67:                          .format(name))\\n68: \\n69: \\n70: class ValidationError(Error):\\n71: \\n72:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised when a value for a config type was invalid.\\n73: \\n74:     Attributes:\\n75:         value: Config value that triggered the error.\\n76:         msg: Additional error message.\\n77:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n78: \\n79:     def __init__(self, value: typing.Any,\\n80:                  msg: typing.Union[str, Exception]) -> None:\\n81:         super().__init__(\\\"Invalid value '{}' - {}\\\".format(value, msg))\\n82:         self.option = None\\n83: \\n84: \\n85: class KeybindingError(Error):\\n86: \\n87:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised for issues with keybindings.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n88: \\n89: \\n90: class NoOptionError(Error):\\n91: \\n92:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised when an option was not found.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n93: \\n94:     def __init__(self, option: str, *,\\n95:                  deleted: bool = False,\\n96:                  renamed: str = None) -> None:\\n97:         if deleted:\\n98:             assert renamed is None\\n99:             suffix = ' (this option was removed from qutebrowser)'\\n100:         elif renamed is not None:\\n101:             suffix = ' (this option was renamed to {!r})'.format(renamed)\\n102:         else:\\n103:             suffix = ''\\n104: \\n105:         super().__init__(\\\"No option {!r}{}\\\".format(option, suffix))\\n106:         self.option = option\\n107: \\n108: \\n109: @attr.s\\n110: class ConfigErrorDesc:\\n111: \\n112:     \\\"\\\"\\\"A description of an error happening while reading the config.\\n113: \\n114:     Attributes:\\n115:         text: The text to show.\\n116:         exception: The exception which happened.\\n117:         traceback: The formatted traceback of the exception.\\n118:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n119: \\n120:     text = attr.ib()  # type: str\\n121:     exception = attr.ib()  # type: typing.Union[str, Exception]\\n122:     traceback = attr.ib(None)  # type: str\\n123: \\n124:     def __str__(self) -> str:\\n125:         if self.traceback:\\n126:             return '{} - {}: {}'.format(self.text,\\n127:                                         self.exception.__class__.__name__,\\n128:                                         self.exception)\\n129:         return '{}: {}'.format(self.text, self.exception)\\n130: \\n131:     def with_text(self, text: str) -> 'ConfigErrorDesc':\\n132:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get a new ConfigErrorDesc with the given text appended.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n133:         return self.__class__(text='{} ({})'.format(self.text, text),\\n134:                               exception=self.exception,\\n135:                               traceback=self.traceback)\\n136: \\n137: \\n138: class ConfigFileErrors(Error):\\n139: \\n140:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised when multiple errors occurred inside the config.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n141: \\n142:     def __init__(self,\\n143:                  basename: str,\\n144:                  errors: typing.Sequence[ConfigErrorDesc]) -> None:\\n145:         super().__init__(\\\"Errors occurred while reading {}:\\\\n{}\\\".format(\\n146:             basename, '\\\\n'.join('  {}'.format(e) for e in errors)))\\n147:         self.basename = basename\\n148:         self.errors = errors\\n149: \\n150:     def to_html(self) -> str:\\n151:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get the error texts as a HTML snippet.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n152:         template = jinja.environment.from_string(\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n153:         Errors occurred while reading {{ basename }}:\\n154: \\n155:         <ul>\\n156:           {% for error in errors %}\\n157:             <li>\\n158:               <b>{{ error.text }}</b>: {{ error.exception }}\\n159:               {% if error.traceback != none %}\\n160:                 <pre>\\n161:         \\\"\\\"\\\".rstrip() + \\\"\\\\n{{ error.traceback }}\\\" + \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n162:                 </pre>\\n163:               {% endif %}\\n164:             </li>\\n165:           {% endfor %}\\n166:         </ul>\\n167:         \\\"\\\"\\\")\\n168:         return template.render(basename=self.basename, errors=self.errors)\\n169: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/qutebrowser--6b320dc1@1fb0be5039e688ee1f459e2e458fbf8c22a642e6/-/blob/qutebrowser/config/configexc.py\"}"
50. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:\n\n# Copyright 2018-2019 Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>\n#\n# This file is part of qutebrowser.\n#\n# qutebrowser is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify\n# it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by\n# the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or\n# (at your option) any later version.\n#\n# qutebrowser is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,\n# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of\n# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the\n# GNU General Public License for more details.\n#\n# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\n# along with qutebrowser.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\n\n\n\"\"\"Utilities and data structures used by various config code.\"\"\"\n\n\nimport typing\n\nimport attr\nfrom PyQt5.QtCore import QUrl\n\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import utils, urlmatch\nfrom qutebrowser.config import configexc\n\nMYPY = False\nif MYPY:\n    # pylint: disable=unused-import,useless-suppression\n    from qutebrowser.config import configdata\n\n\nclass Unset:\n\n    \"\"\"Sentinel object.\"\"\"\n\n    __slots__ = ()\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n        return '<UNSET>'\n\n\nUNSET = Unset()\n\n\n@attr.s\nclass ScopedValue:\n\n    \"\"\"A configuration value which is valid for a UrlPattern.\n\n    Attributes:\n        value: The value itself.\n        pattern: The UrlPattern for the value, or None for global values.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    value = attr.ib()  # type: typing.Any\n    pattern = attr.ib()  # type: typing.Optional[urlmatch.UrlPattern]\n\n\nclass Values:\n\n    \"\"\"A collection of values for a single setting.\n\n    Currently, this is a list and iterates through all possible ScopedValues to\n    find matching ones.\n\n    In the future, it should be possible to optimize this by doing\n    pre-selection based on hosts, by making this a dict mapping the\n    non-wildcard part of the host to a list of matching ScopedValues.\n\n    That way, when searching for a setting for sub.example.com, we only have to\n    check 'sub.example.com', 'example.com', '.com' and '' instead of checking\n    all ScopedValues for the given setting.\n\n    Attributes:\n        opt: The Option being customized.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self,\n                 opt: 'configdata.Option',\n                 values: typing.MutableSequence = None) -> None:\n        self.opt = opt\n        self._values = values or []\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n        return utils.get_repr(self, opt=self.opt, values=self._values,\n                              constructor=True)\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get the values as human-readable string.\"\"\"\n        if not self:\n            return '{}: <unchanged>'.format(self.opt.name)\n\n        lines = []\n        for scoped in self._values:\n            str_value = self.opt.typ.to_str(scoped.value)\n            if scoped.pattern is None:\n                lines.append('{} = {}'.format(self.opt.name, str_value))\n            else:\n                lines.append('{}: {} = {}'.format(\n                    scoped.pattern, self.opt.name, str_value))\n        return '\\n'.join(lines)\n\n    def __iter__(self) -> typing.Iterator['ScopedValue']:\n        \"\"\"Yield ScopedValue elements.\n\n        This yields in \"normal\" order, i.e. global and then first-set settings\n        first.\n        \"\"\"\n        yield from self._values\n\n    def __bool__(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check whether this value is customized.\"\"\"\n        return bool(self._values)\n\n    def _check_pattern_support(\n            self, arg: typing.Optional[urlmatch.UrlPattern]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Make sure patterns are supported if one was given.\"\"\"\n        if arg is not None and not self.opt.supports_pattern:\n            raise configexc.NoPatternError(self.opt.name)\n\n    def add(self, value: typing.Any,\n            pattern: urlmatch.UrlPattern = None) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Add a value with the given pattern to the list of values.\"\"\"\n        self._check_pattern_support(pattern)\n        self.remove(pattern)\n        scoped = ScopedValue(value, pattern)\n        self._values.append(scoped)\n\n    def remove(self, pattern: urlmatch.UrlPattern = None) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Remove the value with the given pattern.\n\n        If a matching pattern was removed, True is returned.\n        If no matching pattern was found, False is returned.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._check_pattern_support(pattern)\n        old_len = len(self._values)\n        self._values = [v for v in self._values if v.pattern != pattern]\n        return old_len != len(self._values)\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear all customization for this value.\"\"\"\n        self._values = []\n\n    def _get_fallback(self, fallback: typing.Any) -> typing.Any:\n        \"\"\"Get the fallback global/default value.\"\"\"\n        for scoped in self._values:\n            if scoped.pattern is None:\n                return scoped.value\n\n        if fallback:\n            return self.opt.default\n        else:\n            return UNSET\n\n    def get_for_url(self, url: QUrl = None, *,\n                    fallback: bool = True) -> typing.Any:\n        \"\"\"Get a config value, falling back when needed.\n\n        This first tries to find a value matching the URL (if given).\n        If there's no match:\n          With fallback=True, the global/default setting is returned.\n          With fallback=False, UNSET is returned.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._check_pattern_support(url)\n        if url is not None:\n            for scoped in reversed(self._values):\n                if scoped.pattern is not None and scoped.pattern.matches(url):\n                    return scoped.value\n\n            if not fallback:\n                return UNSET\n\n        return self._get_fallback(fallback)\n\n    def get_for_pattern(self,\n                        pattern: typing.Optional[urlmatch.UrlPattern], *,\n                        fallback: bool = True) -> typing.Any:\n        \"\"\"Get a value only if it's been overridden for the given pattern.\n\n        This is useful when showing values to the user.\n\n        If there's no match:\n          With fallback=True, the global/default setting is returned.\n          With fallback=False, UNSET is returned.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._check_pattern_support(pattern)\n        if pattern is not None:\n            for scoped in reversed(self._values):\n                if scoped.pattern == pattern:\n                    return scoped.value\n\n            if not fallback:\n                return UNSET\n\n        return self._get_fallback(fallback)\n",
  "file_path": "/app/qutebrowser/config/configutils.py"
}
Output
"Error: File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it."
51. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:\n\n# Copyright 2014-2019 Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>\n#\n# This file is part of qutebrowser.\n#\n# qutebrowser is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify\n# it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by\n# the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or\n# (at your option) any later version.\n#\n# qutebrowser is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,\n# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of\n# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the\n# GNU General Public License for more details.\n#\n# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\n# along with qutebrowser.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\n\n\"\"\"Exceptions related to config parsing.\"\"\"\n\nimport typing\nimport attr\n\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import jinja, usertypes\n\n\nclass Error(Exception):\n\n    \"\"\"Base exception for config-related errors.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass NoAutoconfigError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when this option can't be set in autoconfig.yml.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, name: str) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\"The {} setting can only be set in config.py!\"\n                         .format(name))\n\n\nclass BackendError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when this setting is unavailable with the current backend.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n            self, name: str,\n            backend: usertypes.Backend,\n            raw_backends: typing.Optional[typing.Mapping[str, bool]]\n    ) -> None:\n        if raw_backends is None or not raw_backends[backend.name]:\n            msg = (\"The {} setting is not available with the {} backend!\"\n                   .format(name, backend.name))\n        else:\n            msg = (\"The {} setting needs {} with the {} backend!\"\n                   .format(name, raw_backends[backend.name], backend.name))\n\n        super().__init__(msg)\n\n\nclass NoPatternError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when the given setting does not support URL patterns.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, name: str) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\"The {} setting does not support URL patterns!\"\n                         .format(name))\n\n\nclass ValidationError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when a value for a config type was invalid.\n\n    Attributes:\n        value: Config value that triggered the error.\n        msg: Additional error message.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, value: typing.Any,\n                 msg: typing.Union[str, Exception]) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\"Invalid value '{}' - {}\".format(value, msg))\n        self.option = None\n\n\nclass KeybindingError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised for issues with keybindings.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass NoOptionError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when an option was not found.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, option: str, *,\n                 deleted: bool = False,\n                 renamed: str = None) -> None:\n        if deleted:\n            assert renamed is None\n            suffix = ' (this option was removed from qutebrowser)'\n        elif renamed is not None:\n            suffix = ' (this option was renamed to {!r})'.format(renamed)\n        else:\n            suffix = ''\n\n        super().__init__(\"No option {!r}{}\".format(option, suffix))\n        self.option = option\n\n\n@attr.s\nclass ConfigErrorDesc:\n\n    \"\"\"A description of an error happening while reading the config.\n\n    Attributes:\n        text: The text to show.\n        exception: The exception which happened.\n        traceback: The formatted traceback of the exception.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    text = attr.ib()  # type: str\n    exception = attr.ib()  # type: typing.Union[str, Exception]\n    traceback = attr.ib(None)  # type: str\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:\n        if self.traceback:\n            return '{} - {}: {}'.format(self.text,\n                                        self.exception.__class__.__name__,\n                                        self.exception)\n        return '{}: {}'.format(self.text, self.exception)\n\n    def with_text(self, text: str) -> 'ConfigErrorDesc':\n        \"\"\"Get a new ConfigErrorDesc with the given text appended.\"\"\"\n        return self.__class__(text='{} ({})'.format(self.text, text),\n                              exception=self.exception,\n                              traceback=self.traceback)\n\n\nclass ConfigFileErrors(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when multiple errors occurred inside the config.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self,\n                 basename: str,\n                 errors: typing.Sequence[ConfigErrorDesc]) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\"Errors occurred while reading {}:\\n{}\".format(\n            basename, '\\n'.join('  {}'.format(e) for e in errors)))\n        self.basename = basename\n        self.errors = errors\n\n    def to_html(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get the error texts as a HTML snippet.\"\"\"\n        template = jinja.environment.from_string(\"\"\"\n        Errors occurred while reading {{ basename }}:\n\n        <ul>\n          {% for error in errors %}\n            <li>\n              <b>{{ error.text }}</b>: {{ error.exception }}\n              {% if error.traceback != none %}\n                <pre>\n        \"\"\".rstrip() + \"\\n{{ error.traceback }}\" + \"\"\"\n                </pre>\n              {% endif %}\n            </li>\n          {% endfor %}\n        </ul>\n        \"\"\")\n        return template.render(basename=self.basename, errors=self.errors)\n",
  "file_path": "/app/qutebrowser/config/configexc.py"
}
Output
"Error: File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it."
52. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "mkdir -p /app/qutebrowser/config",
  "description": "Create the directory structure for config files"
}
Output
""
53. Bash @ -
Input

  "command": "cat > /app/qutebrowser/config/configutils.py << 'EOF'\n# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:\n\n# Copyright 2018-2019 Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>\n#\n# This file is part of qutebrowser.\n#\n# qutebrowser is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify\n# it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by\n# the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or\n# (at your option) any later version.\n#\n# qutebrowser is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,\n# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of\n# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the\n# GNU General Public License for more details.\n#\n# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\n# along with qutebrowser.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\n\n\n\"\"\"Utilities and data structures used by various config code.\"\"\"\n\n\nimport typing\n\nimport attr\nfrom PyQt5.QtCore import QUrl\n\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import utils, urlmatch\nfrom qutebrowser.config import configexc\n\nMYPY = False\nif MYPY:\n    # pylint: disable=unused-import,useless-suppression\n    from qutebrowser.config import configdata\n\n\nclass Unset:\n\n    \"\"\"Sentinel object.\"\"\"\n\n    __slots__ = ()\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n        return '<UNSET>'\n\n\nUNSET = Unset()\n\n\n@attr.s\nclass ScopedValue:\n\n    \"\"\"A configuration value which is valid for a UrlPattern.\n\n    Attributes:\n        value: The value itself.\n        pattern: The UrlPattern for the value, or None for global values.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    value = attr.ib()  # type: typing.Any\n    pattern = attr.ib()  # type: typing.Optional[urlmatch.UrlPattern]\n\n\nclass Values:\n\n    \"\"\"A collection of values for a single setting.\n\n    Currently, this is a list and iterates through all possible ScopedValues to\n    find matching ones.\n\n    In the future, it should be possible to optimize this by doing\n    pre-selection based on hosts, by making this a dict mapping the\n    non-wildcard part of the host to a list of matching ScopedValues.\n\n    That way, when searching for a setting for sub.example.com, we only have to\n    check 'sub.example.com', 'example.com', '.com' and '' instead of checking\n    all ScopedValues for the given setting.\n\n    Attributes:\n        opt: The Option being customized.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self,\n                 opt: 'configdata.Option',\n                 values: typing.MutableSequence = None) -> None:\n        self.opt = opt\n        self._values = values or []\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n        return utils.get_repr(self, opt=self.opt, values=self._values,\n                              constructor=True)\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get the values as human-readable string.\"\"\"\n        if not self:\n            return '{}: <unchanged>'.format(self.opt.name)\n\n        lines = []\n        for scoped in self._values:\n            str_value = self.opt.typ.to_str(scoped.value)\n            if scoped.pattern is None:\n                lines.append('{} = {}'.format(self.opt.name, str_value))\n            else:\n                lines.append('{}: {} = {}'.format(\n                    scoped.pattern, self.opt.name, str_value))\n        return '\\n'.join(lines)\n\n    def __iter__(self) -> typing.Iterator['ScopedValue']:\n        \"\"\"Yield ScopedValue elements.\n\n        This yields in \"normal\" order, i.e. global and then first-set settings\n        first.\n        \"\"\"\n        yield from self._values\n\n    def __bool__(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check whether this value is customized.\"\"\"\n        return bool(self._values)\n\n    def _check_pattern_support(\n            self, arg: typing.Optional[urlmatch.UrlPattern]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Make sure patterns are supported if one was given.\"\"\"\n        if arg is not None and not self.opt.supports_pattern:\n            raise configexc.NoPatternError(self.opt.name)\n\n    def add(self, value: typing.Any,\n            pattern: urlmatch.UrlPattern = None) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Add a value with the given pattern to the list of values.\"\"\"\n        self._check_pattern_support(pattern)\n        self.remove(pattern)\n        scoped = ScopedValue(value, pattern)\n        self._values.append(scoped)\n\n    def remove(self, pattern: urlmatch.UrlPattern = None) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Remove the value with the given pattern.\n\n        If a matching pattern was removed, True is returned.\n        If no matching pattern was found, False is returned.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._check_pattern_support(pattern)\n        old_len = len(self._values)\n        self._values = [v for v in self._values if v.pattern != pattern]\n        return old_len != len(self._values)\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clear all customization for this value.\"\"\"\n        self._values = []\n\n    def _get_fallback(self, fallback: typing.Any) -> typing.Any:\n        \"\"\"Get the fallback global/default value.\"\"\"\n        for scoped in self._values:\n            if scoped.pattern is None:\n                return scoped.value\n\n        if fallback:\n            return self.opt.default\n        else:\n            return UNSET\n\n    def get_for_url(self, url: QUrl = None, *,\n                    fallback: bool = True) -> typing.Any:\n        \"\"\"Get a config value, falling back when needed.\n\n        This first tries to find a value matching the URL (if given).\n        If there's no match:\n          With fallback=True, the global/default setting is returned.\n          With fallback=False, UNSET is returned.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._check_pattern_support(url)\n        if url is not None:\n            for scoped in reversed(self._values):\n                if scoped.pattern is not None and scoped.pattern.matches(url):\n                    return scoped.value\n\n            if not fallback:\n                return UNSET\n\n        return self._get_fallback(fallback)\n\n    def get_for_pattern(self,\n                        pattern: typing.Optional[urlmatch.UrlPattern], *,\n                        fallback: bool = True) -> typing.Any:\n        \"\"\"Get a value only if it's been overridden for the given pattern.\n\n        This is useful when showing values to the user.\n\n        If there's no match:\n          With fallback=True, the global/default setting is returned.\n          With fallback=False, UNSET is returned.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._check_pattern_support(pattern)\n        if pattern is not None:\n            for scoped in reversed(self._values):\n                if scoped.pattern == pattern:\n                    return scoped.value\n\n            if not fallback:\n                return UNSET\n\n        return self._get_fallback(fallback)\nEOF\n",
  "description": "Write configutils.py file"
}

54. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /app/qutebrowser/config/configexc.py << 'EOF'\n# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:\n\n# Copyright 2014-2019 Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>\n#\n# This file is part of qutebrowser.\n#\n# qutebrowser is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify\n# it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by\n# the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or\n# (at your option) any later version.\n#\n# qutebrowser is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,\n# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of\n# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the\n# GNU General Public License for more details.\n#\n# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\n# along with qutebrowser.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\n\n\"\"\"Exceptions related to config parsing.\"\"\"\n\nimport typing\nimport attr\n\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import jinja, usertypes\n\n\nclass Error(Exception):\n\n    \"\"\"Base exception for config-related errors.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass NoAutoconfigError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when this option can't be set in autoconfig.yml.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, name: str) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\"The {} setting can only be set in config.py!\"\n                         .format(name))\n\n\nclass BackendError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when this setting is unavailable with the current backend.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n            self, name: str,\n            backend: usertypes.Backend,\n            raw_backends: typing.Optional[typing.Mapping[str, bool]]\n    ) -> None:\n        if raw_backends is None or not raw_backends[backend.name]:\n            msg = (\"The {} setting is not available with the {} backend!\"\n                   .format(name, backend.name))\n        else:\n            msg = (\"The {} setting needs {} with the {} backend!\"\n                   .format(name, raw_backends[backend.name], backend.name))\n\n        super().__init__(msg)\n\n\nclass NoPatternError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when the given setting does not support URL patterns.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, name: str) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\"The {} setting does not support URL patterns!\"\n                         .format(name))\n\n\nclass ValidationError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when a value for a config type was invalid.\n\n    Attributes:\n        value: Config value that triggered the error.\n        msg: Additional error message.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, value: typing.Any,\n                 msg: typing.Union[str, Exception]) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\"Invalid value '{}' - {}\".format(value, msg))\n        self.option = None\n\n\nclass KeybindingError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised for issues with keybindings.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass NoOptionError(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when an option was not found.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, option: str, *,\n                 deleted: bool = False,\n                 renamed: str = None) -> None:\n        if deleted:\n            assert renamed is None\n            suffix = ' (this option was removed from qutebrowser)'\n        elif renamed is not None:\n            suffix = ' (this option was renamed to {!r})'.format(renamed)\n        else:\n            suffix = ''\n\n        super().__init__(\"No option {!r}{}\".format(option, suffix))\n        self.option = option\n\n\n@attr.s\nclass ConfigErrorDesc:\n\n    \"\"\"A description of an error happening while reading the config.\n\n    Attributes:\n        text: The text to show.\n        exception: The exception which happened.\n        traceback: The formatted traceback of the exception.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    text = attr.ib()  # type: str\n    exception = attr.ib()  # type: typing.Union[str, Exception]\n    traceback = attr.ib(None)  # type: str\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:\n        if self.traceback:\n            return '{} - {}: {}'.format(self.text,\n                                        self.exception.__class__.__name__,\n                                        self.exception)\n        return '{}: {}'.format(self.text, self.exception)\n\n    def with_text(self, text: str) -> 'ConfigErrorDesc':\n        \"\"\"Get a new ConfigErrorDesc with the given text appended.\"\"\"\n        return self.__class__(text='{} ({})'.format(self.text, text),\n                              exception=self.exception,\n                              traceback=self.traceback)\n\n\nclass ConfigFileErrors(Error):\n\n    \"\"\"Raised when multiple errors occurred inside the config.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self,\n                 basename: str,\n                 errors: typing.Sequence[ConfigErrorDesc]) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\"Errors occurred while reading {}:\\n{}\".format(\n            basename, '\\n'.join('  {}'.format(e) for e in errors)))\n        self.basename = basename\n        self.errors = errors\n\n    def to_html(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get the error texts as a HTML snippet.\"\"\"\n        template = jinja.environment.from_string(\"\"\"\n        Errors occurred while reading {{ basename }}:\n\n        <ul>\n          {% for error in errors %}\n            <li>\n              <b>{{ error.text }}</b>: {{ error.exception }}\n              {% if error.traceback != none %}\n                <pre>\n        \"\"\".rstrip() + \"\\n{{ error.traceback }}\" + \"\"\"\n                </pre>\n              {% endif %}\n            </li>\n          {% endfor %}\n        </ul>\n        \"\"\")\n        return template.render(basename=self.basename, errors=self.errors)\nEOF\n",
  "description": "Write configexc.py file"
}
Output
""
